title: Letter to Oklahoma Senators From a Post-Abortive Mother
author: Sonya Gonella
orientation: An open letter from a self-described post-abortive mother urging Oklahoma's Senate Health and Human Services Committee to stop using women like her as a justification for voting down SB495, an equal-protection bill that would have established abortion as criminal homicide. The author argues that the committee's 'protect women from criminalization' rationale is false chivalry and false compassion that hates the preborn to death.
audiences: Oklahoma state senators on the HHS Committee
Pro-life Christians wary of criminalizing women
Post-abortive women considering the abolitionist position
Pastors weighing biblical justice and repentance

questions.question: Why does the author insist she is not asking to be excluded from criminal liability for her past abortion?
questions.audience: Oklahoma state senators on the HHS Committee
questions.answer: She views her own past abortion as participation in murder for which she has been forgiven by Christ, not as a victim status that should shield future women from accountability. She wants legislators to stop weaponizing women like her as a rhetorical reason to deny equal protection to the preborn.
questions.quote: I am writing to you as a woman who has participated in murder by abortion.  By God's grace, I am a repentant murderer redeemed by the Lord Jesus Christ! As a woman that falls in the camp of those that you refuse to “criminalize”, as demonstrated by your 10-0 vote on SB495, this is a serious and urgent appeal to each one of you to CEASE from using women, like myself, as justification to deny equal protection to the preborn.

questions.question: How does the author rebut the claim that equal-protection bills 'criminalize women'?
questions.audience: Pro-life Christians wary of criminalizing women
questions.answer: She argues equal protection does not single women out at all; it criminalizes the act of murder by abortion regardless of who commits it, and existing statutes already cover women who are genuinely coerced or victimized.
questions.quote: Equal protection under the law does NOT single out women for criminalization. It establishes the ACT of murder, by abortion, as criminal.  I fully and ONLY support bills like SB495. I was prepared to be “criminalized as a woman” long before I knew it would not be a retroactive law. There is no ‘right’ for anyone to unjustly shed the innocent blood of our sons and daughters. Laws already exist to protect/provide equal justice for women truly coerced and victimized.

questions.question: What does the author mean when she calls the committee's posture 'false chivalry'?
questions.audience: Pastors weighing biblical justice and repentance
questions.answer: True chivalry, she says, is courage and readiness to defend the truly weak. By casting women seeking abortions as the weak who need protection from prosecution, the committee inverts chivalry and abandons the actually defenseless party, the preborn child being taken to slaughter.
questions.quote: CHIVALRY includes the combination of qualities…especially courage, honor, courtesy, justice, and a readiness to help the weak – – – Sadly, the committee is holding up women as the weak in need of “protection” from “criminalization” versus a readiness to help the truly weak — the preborn who cannot speak and are being taken to slaughter.  This is not praiseworthy.

questions.question: Why does the author call the committee's vote 'false compassion' rather than mercy toward women?
questions.audience: Post-abortive women considering the abolitionist position
questions.answer: She defines compassion as recognizing suffering and acting to relieve it. Killing the bill, she argues, refuses to act for the suffering preborn and shows partiality toward those who kill them, which is not mercy but injustice dressed up as kindness.
questions.quote: COMPASSION is to recognize the suffering of others and then take action to help – – – By refusing a bill like SB 495, the committee is not taking appropriate action in recognizing the suffering of the innocent preborn but rather it is practicing the sin of partiality towards those who murder them.

questions.question: How does the author respond to the idea that leaving abortion legal spares women from suffering?
questions.audience: Post-abortive women considering the abolitionist position
questions.answer: Drawing on her own experience, she says legalized abortion actually multiplies suffering for women, men, children, families, and the broader culture by encouraging sin and the painful consequences of rebellion against God, and that real relief came only through repentance and the cross of Christ.
questions.quote: In showing preference to those involved in their murder, we are hating our preborn neighbors to death. It actually promotes the “suffering” of women, men, children, extended family and our entire culture by encouraging sin and letting us live with the painful consequences of rebellion before a Holy God. I wouldn't wish that on anyone. I know because so once was I until someone took me, as a sinner, to the cross of Christ and the sufficiency of His shed blood to forgive my sins.

questions.question: What theological framing does the author apply to the senators' 10-0 vote?
questions.audience: Pastors weighing biblical justice and repentance
questions.answer: She frames the unanimous vote as resisting God Himself, invoking the language of Acts 9 ('kicking against the goads') and Romans 13 (the civil magistrate as God's servant to punish wrongdoing) to insist that legislators are not the standard of good and evil but accountable to it.
questions.quote: Ultimately your 10-0 vote is kicking against the goads of Almighty God to Whom we all must give an account.  We have a space of grace to change our ways…let's not take that grace of God in vain! You are God's servant, an agent of retribution to the wrongdoer…sent by Him to punish those who do wrong and to praise those who do right. He determines the standard for good and evildoing — not us.

questions.question: What specific call does the author leave with the senators at the end of the letter?
questions.answer: She calls the senators to Godly repentance, to fear God rather than men or women, and to act now while there is still time, framing immediate obedience as the only acceptable response.
questions.quote:
I am praying for you to be granted Godly repentance to see though you may justify yourselves before men… God knows your hearts. For that which is highly esteemed among men is an abomination (detestable) in the sight of God. May He grant you wisdom to fear Him, not man (which includes women), and turn from evil!

NOW is always the right time to do the right thing! Please, don't harden your heart…May you bear fruit in keeping with repentance! Amen.

related.title: BREAKING: SB495 Might be Heard in Senate HHS Committee on Feb. 3!
related.reason: Direct background on the SB495 hearing and 10-0 committee vote that this letter responds to.

related.title: OK Senate HHS Committee Votes in Favor of Murdering 10,000 Babies
related.reason: Free the States' immediate reaction to the same HHS Committee vote that prompted this letter.

related.title: Pro-Life Oklahoman Who Voted to Table the Bill to Abolish Abortion: Julie Daniels
related.reason: Profiles one of the HHS Committee senators the author is appealing to and exposes the 'protect women' rationale for the no-vote.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Names the Oklahoma legislative leaders blocking equal-protection bills like SB495 from advancing.

related.title: Students for Life: Unequal Justice and the 'Legal' DIY Abortion
related.reason: Engages the broader pro-life argument against criminalizing women that the author is rebutting from a post-abortive perspective.

related.title: Incremental Laws of All Sorts Undermine Repentance
related.reason: Develops the same theological argument the author makes: that withholding equal-protection law promotes sin and impedes repentance.

related.title: On Repentance, Abolitionists Must Lead by Example: An Open Letter
related.reason: Parallel open letter centered on repentance, the same posture the author urges on the senators.
